publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-10-10 00:11:54 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: Undetermined, Code for undetermined script script.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Collection includes 44 original, hand-collaged past-up boards from which the IGTimes page layouts were reproduced; hundreds of original manuscript, typescript, and desktop printing documents comprising the text files from which its editorial content was drawn; more than 4000 original photographs of aerosol street art submitted to the magazine by a community of international aerosol writers and readers; ca. 5000 original reader submissions; ca. 500 original magazines and fanzines; 150 clippings from newspapers and magazines; the archive of the Aerosol Art Armada, which includes ca. 1,000 35 mm. slide transparencies; a selection of flyers for parties and openings in New York City; 5 file folders with information from scholarly researchers who contacted the IGTimes for information on street art.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: In late 1983, downtown artist and photographer David Schmidlapp published the first issue of the IGTimes under the name "Yanqui Junkie." The International Graffiti Times was "the original inner-city, international, aerosol 'zine,' and the Aerosol Art Armada traveling show, providing a view of the development of aerosol street art and the rise of hip hop culture in New York City and around the world from the 1970s to the 1990s.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Collection includes 44 original, hand-collaged past-up boards from which the IGTimes page layouts were reproduced; hundreds of original manuscript, typescript, and desktop printing documents comprising the text files from which its editorial content was drawn; more than 4000 original photographs of aerosol street art submitted to the magazine by a community of international aerosol writers and readers; ca. 5000 original reader submissions; ca. 500 original magazines and fanzines; 150 clippings from newspapers and magazines; the archive of the Aerosol Art Armada, which includes ca. 1,000 35 mm. slide transparencies; a selection of flyers for parties and openings in New York City; 5 file folders with information from scholarly researchers who contacted the IGTimes for information on street art.

emph.ref: Series IV. Magazine and Newspaper Clippings

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: International Graffiti Times Archive, #8067. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

did.unittitle: Centre International de Recherches Sur l'anarchisme
Dance Flo Proz: A Coloring Book 4 B-Kids
I'd Rather Be Out Killing Communists

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vol. 1, No. 3, 5; Vol. 3, No. 27; Vol. 7, No. 23

did.unittitle: Nog the Protector of the Pyramides
Aerosol Armada info, fact sheets, bio, letters, and flyers
IGT letters, interviews, drafts, and press

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Rock and Roll, Bronx Museum, NY Press, John Nation, and New Museum.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains original photographs of aerosol street art, many submitted to the magazine by a community of international 'aerosol' writers and other readers, others shot by Schmidlapp and the magazine's inner circle and a few from professional photographers documenting the various scenes.
Many folders also contain flyers and correspondence.

did.unittitle: Visit to West Hollywood and East LA, plus Venice Beach
Michael Lawrence, Trains, USA, black and white
Letters with photos, many written by Cris Mendoza
Series IV. Magazine and Newspaper Clippings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: After Dark, The Albuquerque Tribune, Art and Artists, Arts and Entertainment

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Beat Down, Bayou La Rose, Barricada, Beef

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The City Sun, Classic Films, Criminal Crimes, Dissident News

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: El Salvador's Link, Elevated, Eye, Fallout, Fifth Estate, Flavor

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hip Hop, Heads, It's Yours, K, Kick It Over, Kulcha

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lifelines, L.A.C.E., LA Reader, LA Times, Metro

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: National Crime and Graffiti Prevention News, New City, New Observer, NY Newsday, NY Press, A New World Rising, No Doubt

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Open Road, Oregonian, Paper, Peoples Tribune, Philadelphia Forum

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rap Sheet, RAW, Reader, Reality Now, Red Bass, Resident, Revolt

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: San Francisco Bay News, San Francisco Museum of Art, Small Publisher, Scotland on Sunday

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Tox, Theftway, Vibe, Vilalge Beat, Voice, Yacata, Wax

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of manuscripts, typescript and desktop printing documents comprising the core of the magazine's editorial content for each of the 15 issues, including interview transcripts and notes for articles.

did.unittitle: Xeroxes of press clips and covers of press

A selection of fliers for parties, openings, and events in New York City featuring the magazine itself and members of the IGTimes community, including correspondence with alternative art spaces ABC no Rio, Fashion Moda Gallery, Franklin Furnace and Food Stamp Gallery, ca. 1984-1994.
Flyers, clippings, and sections from zines

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Writer's Resource Guide, War Craft, New Urbanism, Café Armageddon, 14k, Bullshit Monthly

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists mainly of reader submissions including letters, postcards, original artwork, photographs and other items from local and international correspondents, as well as mail art, prisoner and G.I. letters, and subscription requests

did.unittitle: Letters from prisons and some military

IGT correspondence used in "Now Scream" exhibition

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sent to the magazine from artists and institutions including Ryosuke Cohen, Artpool, Vincent Salas and others.

did.unittitle: Letters and postcards sent to IGT
